steps: Drive to the safehouse in Little Havana|Meet your contact at the docks|Lose the cops (1-star wanted level)
tips: Take your time — there's no time limit during the tutorial. Explore the safehouse area for a free weapon pickup.
trivia: The opening mission introduces the protagonists and sets up the core conflict, similar to GTA5's Prologue.

steps: Steal a car from the dealership lot|Lose the heat (2-star wanted level)|Deliver the car to the garage
tips: Use alleyways to break line of sight quickly. Don't shoot — just drive.
trivia: Rockstar traditionally names the protagonist's first car something memorable. This mission establishes the player's starter vehicle.

steps: Go to the nightclub meeting spot|Speak with each crew member to learn their specialties|Drive to the test job location
tips: Talk to everyone — one crew member gives a bonus side mission later if you build rapport.
trivia: Crew members introduced here recur throughout the entire story, similar to GTA5's trio system but with a larger ensemble cast.

steps: Reach the target building in Downtown|Retrieve the package (stealth or loud approach)|Exfiltrate before backup arrives
tips: Stealth is optional but easier — use the side entrance. If you go loud bring body armor.
trivia: This mission teaches basic stealth mechanics and introduces the expanded wanted level system.

steps: Drive to Club Vice on Ocean Drive|Intimidate the club owner into paying up|Collect the protection money from three businesses|Return to your boss
tips: Use a baseball bat instead of a gun — melee intimidation works faster and doesn't trigger a police response.
trivia: The club scene is a direct homage to GTA: Vice City's Malibu Club, which was one of the most iconic locations in the series.

steps: Rendezvous at the marina|Take the speedboat to the offshore exchange point|Fend off the ambush boats|Deliver the cargo to the mainland safehouse
tips: Stay in the speedboat — it's faster than the attackers' dinghies. Use the SMG for drive-by shooting from the boat.
trivia: Boat chases are expected to be a major gameplay element in GTA6, given the Vice City setting with its extensive waterways and coastline.

steps: Follow the target without being spotted|Listen to the meeting from a safe distance|Eliminate the informant before he talks|Lose the resulting wanted level
tips: Use a civilian car for the tail — sports cars are too conspicuous. Park at a distance and use the phone camera to zoom in on the meeting.
trivia: Tail missions are a GTA staple. This one introduces the stealth follow mechanic with an urban Miami backdrop.

steps: Scout the warehouse from the adjacent rooftop|Plant C4 on the three supply crates|Fight through the reinforcements|Escape in the provided getaway car
tips: Bring a rifle for the rooftop scouting phase. Plant C4 on the western crate first — it has the fewest guards nearby.
trivia: The warehouse district is based on Miami's Wynwood and Allapattah industrial areas, known for their street art and warehouses.

steps: Steal the garbage truck from the depot|Collect the three marked bags across town|Dump them in the Everglades|Return the truck without drawing too much attention
tips: Drive carefully — the truck is slow and attracts attention if visibly damaged. Use the compactor to hide evidence if you went loud.
trivia: This mission references the body-disposal mechanics previously seen in GTA5's Trevor Philips missions, expanded for the Vice City setting.

steps: Visit five businesses in Little Haiti|Collect payment from each business owner|Deal with the one refusing to pay|Return to your crew's base
tips: Start with the northern businesses — the southern ones get spooked and are harder to collect from. Breaking one window is usually enough to persuade the holdout.
trivia: Protection racket missions are a Vice City tradition, going back to the original GTA: Vice City's asset system and gang territory mechanics.

steps: Rendezvous with the Cuban contact in Little Havana|Transport the merchandise across town|Survive the gang ambush in the industrial district|Deliver to the warehouse
tips: Don't stop for the ambush — keep driving and let your passenger shoot. If the vehicle takes too much damage, swap cars quickly in the alley.
trivia: Cuban and Haitian gang dynamics were a major theme in the original GTA: Vice City and are expected to return in GTA6, reflecting Miami's real cultural landscape.

steps: Enter the strip club and blend in|Find the target in the VIP room|Follow him after he leaves the club|Confront him at his waterfront mansion
tips: Dress appropriately — wearing a suit reduces suspicion inside. Don't draw your weapon inside the club or you'll trigger an instant 3-star wanted level.
trivia: The strip club is expected to be a recurring hub location, similar to the Vanilla Unicorn in GTA5 but with more interactive interiors.

steps: Set up the roadblock on the I-95 overpass|Stop the armored truck|Crack the safe (drill minigame under time pressure)|Escape through the side roads|Split the take at the safehouse
tips: Place the roadblock after the highway bend — the driver won't see it in time to brake. Use sticky bombs on the back doors if the drill takes too long.
trivia: Highway chases and vehicle takedowns are expected to be a core gameplay loop, building on GTA5's armored car robberies with more cinematic execution.

steps: Take the airboat into the Everglades|Follow the GPS coordinates to the stash house|Eliminate the rival crew guarding it|Collect the drug packages|Return before sunset
tips: Use the airboat — it's the only vehicle that can navigate the shallow swamp water. Alligators are environmental hazards, not enemies — stay out of the water.
trivia: The Everglades are confirmed to be part of GTA6's map, serving as the game's wilderness area similar to GTA5's Blaine County but with a distinct Florida swamp ecosystem.

steps: Meet the corrupt IAB contact at the precinct|Plant evidence in the target's locker|Frame him during the subsequent raid|Escape the internal affairs investigation
tips: Change into the police uniform to move freely inside the station. Don't run — walking speed raises less suspicion. Avoid eye contact with the desk sergeant.
trivia: Corrupt police storylines are a hallmark of GTA narratives, from Officer Tenpenny in San Andreas to Francis McReary in GTA4.

steps: Infiltrate the boatyard at night|Silently take out the patrol guards|Sabotage the three speedboats|Plant the GPS tracker on the main yacht|Exfiltrate via the water
tips: Use suppressed weapons — loud gunfire spawns reinforcements from the main warehouse. Swimming away after sabotage is quieter than running.
trivia: Coastal and maritime locations play a much larger role in GTA6 than any previous GTA title, reflecting the game's Florida-inspired setting.

steps: Attend the cartel meeting at the luxury hotel|Negotiate the deal (dialogue choices affect outcome)|Survive the double-cross ambush|Chase down the traitor through the city|Recover the stolen product
tips: Dialogue choices during the meeting affect how many enemies you face. The diplomatic approach means fewer guards but a lower initial payout. The aggressive approach means more enemies but you keep all the money.
trivia: Branching dialogue with gameplay consequences is rumored to be a new feature in GTA6, building on Red Dead Redemption 2's dynamic conversation system.

steps: Steal the Coast Guard patrol boat from the station|Intercept the smuggler's ship 3 miles offshore|Board and secure the vessel|Steer it back to your private dock|Unload the cargo
tips: Approach from the east — the patrol boat's lights are less visible against the rising sun. Board quickly; the smugglers will attempt to scuttle the ship if you take too long.
trivia: Offshore missions utilize GTA6's expanded ocean map, which is rumored to be significantly larger than GTA5's maritime area.

steps: Go to the Vice City Mall|Confront the rival gang leader in the food court|Survive the ambush — they knew you were coming|Fight out through the parking garage floor by floor|Escape in any available vehicle
tips: Cover is plentiful but destructible. The food court has the best sightlines. The parking garage exit has a police blockade — use the ramp on level 3 to jump over it.
trivia: Indoor combat in crowded public spaces represents a new technical achievement for the series, with fully detailed mall interiors.

steps: Drive to Vice City International Airport|Steal the cargo plane from the hangar|Fly to the drop zone (stay below radar altitude)|Parachute the cargo to the ground team|Land the plane at the desert airstrip
tips: Fly below 500ft to avoid air patrol radar detection. The cargo drop must land within the yellow circle or you'll have to retrieve it manually from the swamp.
trivia: Aircraft smuggling missions return, with GTA6's map rumored to include multiple airstrips and a much larger flyable airspace than previous games.

steps: Go to the exchange location at the abandoned motel|Negotiate the hostage release|The deal goes wrong — eliminate the kidnappers|Escort the freed hostage to the hospital|Lose the police attention
tips: Use a silenced pistol for the initial shots — if the hostage gets caught in crossfire the mission fails instantly. Park a fast car pointing toward the road before the exchange begins.
trivia: Hostage mechanics may be expanded from GTA5's limited escort missions, potentially involving negotiation dialogue and dynamic NPC behavior.

steps: Travel to the cartel boss's oceanfront mansion|Complete three tasks to earn their trust — Race: win the street race|Collection: repossess the stolen sports car|Enforcement: eliminate the snitch before he testifies
tips: Choices in earlier missions can affect your cartel trust level. Higher trust means better rewards but makes it harder to betray them later.
trivia: The branching faction reputation system is one of the most anticipated features in GTA6, potentially affecting which missions are available and how the story unfolds.

steps: Scout the casino (pose as a high-roller guest)|Choose your approach — stealth (hack the vault) or loud (blast through)|Complete 2-3 setup missions based on your chosen approach|Execute the heist|Escape through the maintenance tunnels
tips: The stealth approach pays more but requires more setup missions. The loud approach is faster but you keep a smaller cut. Scout thoroughly — hidden entrances exist on all four sides of the casino.
trivia: Heists are expected to be GTA6's flagship feature, greatly expanded from GTA5's five-heist structure. Multiple approaches, dynamic loot values, and crew management are anticipated.

steps: Take a jetski to the yacht party offshore|Find and photograph the three incriminating documents|Upload them to your contact via phone|Leave the yacht without raising any alarm
tips: Dress code matters — wear the outfit provided during the setup mission. If you fall in the water, you'll need to change clothes or the guards get suspicious of your wet appearance.
trivia: Social stealth — blending into crowds and events — is expected to be a significant new mechanic in GTA6, inspired by Red Dead Redemption 2's witness and disguise systems.

steps: Register at the underground street race meet|Win three qualifying heats to advance|Win the final race for the grand prize|Optional: beat the champion's time attack record
tips: Use a custom-tuned sports car — stock vehicles can't compete at higher difficulties. Nitrous is available but limited — save it for the final straightaway of each race.
trivia: Street racing is expected to return as a major side activity, with an expanded car customization system that may rival or surpass GTA5's Los Santos Customs.

steps: Assemble the crew at the staging point|Approach the rival kingpin's oceanfront mansion|Suppress the exterior guards (watch for snipers on the roof)|Breach the front gate with C4 or a battering ram|Clear the mansion room by room|Confront the rival boss (player choice: kill or spare)
tips: Bring heavy weapons — the mansion has RPG-wielding guards on the upper floors. If you spare the boss, he becomes a contact for future missions but may betray you later. Killing him gives an immediate cash bonus.
trivia: Mansion interiors are expected to be fully explorable without loading screens, a significant technical leap from GTA5 where most interiors were separate instances.

steps: Set up on the highway overpass with your crew|Stop the lead escort vehicle (EMP device or sniper)|Take out the reinforcement trucks|Secure the target truck|Drive it to the chop shop before the timer runs out
tips: Disable the lead vehicle first — the entire convoy stops dead without it. Taking out the helicopter early prevents it from radioing for overwhelming backup.
trivia: The presence of military-grade hardware like EMP devices in civilian missions suggests a more militarized late-game, similar to GTA Online's escalation in its later updates.

steps: Meet with your crew one last time|Choose your endgame approach — Option A: eliminate the FIB contact (stealth)|Option B: rob the Federal Reserve (loud assault)|Option C: play both sides against each other (most complex)|Complete the first phase of your chosen path
tips: Make a manual save before choosing — this is the point of no return. Option C requires the highest crew stats across the board but yields the best outcome for all characters involved.
trivia: Multiple endings based on choices made throughout the game are strongly rumored for GTA6, with a more nuanced system than GTA5's three-option endgame choice.

steps: Infiltrate the FIB headquarters downtown|Plant the virus in the server room on floor 12|Download the evidence files|Destroy the physical backup servers|Fight through the FIB tactical response teams|Escape via the helicopter on the roof
tips: Bring the heavy sniper for the rooftop section. The server room is on floor 12 — elevators are disabled once the alarm triggers, so take the stairs. Save at least one grenade for the helicopter escort on the roof.
trivia: The FIB (GTA universe's version of the FBI) has been a recurring antagonist organization since GTA5, first appearing as a major faction in the series' HD era.

steps: Drill into the underground vault from the storm drain|Manage the thermal drill while waves of cops attack|Load the gold bars into the freight elevator|Escape through the subway tunnel network|Surface at the designated extraction point|Drive to the buyer's warehouse to fence the gold
tips: The drill attracts waves of cops — set up proximity mines in the tunnel approaches beforehand. The subway escape has three possible routes; use the one you scouted during the setup mission for the smoothest exit.
trivia: The Federal Reserve heist is expected to be GTA6's equivalent of GTA5's Union Depository — the single biggest score in the entire game.

steps: Confront the traitor at the Port of Vice City|Survive the ambush (they knew you were coming)|Chase the traitor through the container yard|Final confrontation (interactive cutscene with QTEs)|Decide the traitor's fate — and live with the consequences
tips: The outcome depends on who you sided with and helped throughout the game. Some crew members may not survive this mission if you neglected their loyalty missions earlier.
trivia: GTA6's rumored loyalty and relationship system means side missions with crew members directly impact who betrays you, who survives, and which ending you can access.

steps: Drive through Vice City one last time|Visit key locations from your journey — the places change based on your choices|Return to the safehouse|Watch the ending cutscene
tips: Just enjoy the ride. This is a thematic victory lap — no combat, no timers, no pressure. The locations you visit and the characters you see depend on which ending path you chose.
trivia: GTA games traditionally end with a reflective moment. GTA6 is expected to have multiple epilogue variations that show the consequences of your major story decisions.
